steps.title: Identify the exponential function form
steps.content:
The function is $f(t) = 28,000(2)^t$. In the standard exponential form $f(t) = a \cdot b^t$:
  • The coefficient $a = 28,000$ represents the initial value (the population when $t = 0$)
  • The base $b = 2$ is the growth factor (bacteria double every hour)

steps.title: Evaluate at t = 0 to find the initial value
steps.content:
Since $t$ represents hours after the initial measurement, the initial measurement corresponds to $t = 0$.

Evaluate the function:
$$f(0) = 28,000(2)^0 = 28,000 \cdot 1 = 28,000$$
This shows that 28,000 is the bacterial population at the initial measurement.

That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
